summary: Matched fn_8003F654 in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c and promoted plbonuslib.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The change rewrote the function with codegen-oriented local variable ordering/control flow, added a local inline my_sqrtf using __frsqrte/Newton iterations, removed the unused MSL/math_ppc.h include, and verified the whole translation unit at 100% for .text, .data, and .sdata2.

key_files.reason: Matched fn_8003F654, added my_sqrtf helper, removed MSL/math_ppc.h include, and adjusted local/control-flow structure for codegen.
Changed melee/pl/plbonuslib.c object classification from NonMatching to Matching.

what_changed: configure.py now marks Object(Matching, "melee/pl/plbonuslib.c") instead of Object(NonMatching, "melee/pl/plbonuslib.c").
Added static inline float my_sqrtf(float x), implemented with __frsqrte on a double cast and three Newton refinement steps using local double constants half = 0.5 and three = 3.0.
Removed #include <MSL/math_ppc.h>; the PR body states this avoided disturbing the generated .sdata2 layout.

Reworked fn_8003F654 from a semantically clear but nonmatching version into a matching implementation using renamed locals, explicit branches for absolute values, helper calls pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC, pl_CheckIfSameTeam, and pl_CalculateAverage, and stack Vec3 locals other_pos and cam_pos.
Distance calculation in the type==1 path now uses my_sqrtf instead of sqrtf to match target math codegen.
Average updates for xD8C and xD94 were rewritten through pl_CalculateAverage rather than direct division expressions.
Verification reported by the PR: python configure.py --require-protos, ninja, fn_8003F654 at 100.0%, and plbonuslib.c .text/.data/.sdata2 all 100.0%.
smart_moves: Used local double constants inside my_sqrtf instead of adding extra file-scope constants, preserving .sdata2 layout while still matching sqrt codegen.
Removed an unused math_ppc include because even unused includes can affect generated local statics or section layout in this codebase.
Matched at translation-unit level, not only function level: after fn_8003F654 matched, the PR linked plbonuslib.c as Matching and verified .text, .data, and .sdata2.
Introduced small wrapper/helper usage such as pl_CalculateAverage and pl_CheckIfSameTeam where the target codegen apparently favored call structure over inlined arithmetic/conditions.
Kept codegen-sensitive temporaries such as new_var, b34_result, prev_pos2, pos_x2, and abs rather than simplifying them away, which is often necessary for stack/register matching.
decomp_lessons: For math-heavy matching, replacing a library sqrtf call with an inline __frsqrte-based implementation can be necessary when the original target used PPC reciprocal-square-root codegen.
Local constants can be a section-layout tactic: putting half and three inside the helper avoided adding extra .sdata2 local statics according to the PR body.
Do not assume unused includes are harmless; header changes can affect emitted constants and .sdata2 matching.
When promoting a file to Matching, verify every relevant section, not just the newly matched function.
Average/absolute-value expressions may need to be written in verbose branch form to reproduce original compiler output.
Wrapper functions such as pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC, pl_CheckIfSameTeam, and pl_CalculateAverage can improve matching by matching original call boundaries and expression structure.
naming_conventions: Matched helper uses project-style static inline lowercase name my_sqrtf, but evidence does not show whether this is an intended final symbol name or a temporary matching helper.
Player/stale-move table fields retain offset-style names such as xD74, xD78, xD7C, xD80, xD84, xD88, xD8C, xD90, xD94, and xD98.
Existing decomp placeholder/function names remain, including fn_8003F654 and fn_8003F53C.
The rewrite favored descriptive locals like table, entity, teammate_slot, teammate_table, other_pos, and cam_pos alongside codegen-driven temporaries like new_var, b34_result, and abs.
Type naming uses project types such as pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, Fighter_GObj, and Vec3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline PPC sqrt approximation: __frsqrte((double)x), three Newton iterations, then y = (float)(x * guess) through a volatile float.
Used local double constants half and three to avoid extra .sdata2 local statics.
Inserted u8 _[4] = { 0 }; and volatile float y in my_sqrtf, likely for stack/codegen shape; exact reviewer rationale is not present.
Removed MSL/math_ppc.h to keep .sdata2 layout matching.
Replaced direct sqrtf(dist) with my_sqrtf(expression) for the distance computation.
Expanded ternary absolute-value operations into explicit if/else assignments, including no-op unary plus assignments, to match generated branches.
Used intermediate temporaries and preserved redundant-looking aliases such as prev_pos2 = prevPos and b34 = b34_result for codegen.
Changed loop and condition shape for the six-player scan to a nested/compound condition using pl_CheckIfSameTeam(slot, i).
Replaced direct division-average formulas with pl_CalculateAverage calls for matching.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the supplied PR context.
The PR body explicitly documented why local double constants and include removal mattered for .sdata2 matching.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that add local inline sqrt helpers using __frsqrte for matching.
Search for cases where removing an unused include fixed .sdata2 layout.
Look for other plbonuslib.c matching PRs involving stale move table fields xD74 through xD98.
Check whether my_sqrtf was later renamed, shared, or replaced by a common helper.
Find uses of pl_CalculateAverage and pl_CheckIfSameTeam introduced specifically for codegen matching.
